IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: SEE PAGE 2, PART III, LINE 1 FOR THE ORGANIZATION'S MISSION.
IRS990.MissionDesc: WITHIN THE RELIGIOUS SOCIETY OF FRIENDS ARE MANY ORGANIZATIONS REQUIRING FINANCIAL SERVICES TO SUPPORT THEIR WORK. AMONG THESE ORGANIZATIONS ARE FRIENDS MEETINGS, CHURCHES, SCHOOLS, RETIREMENT COMMUNITIES AND OTHER NON-PROFIT FRIENDS GROUPS WHOSE PURPOSE IS TO ADVANCE SOME ASPECT OF THE WORK AND FAITH OF THE RELIGIOUS SOCIETY OF FRIENDS. FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION WAS CREATED TO MEET THIS NEED. MORE SPECIFICALLY, THE PURPOSES OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION ARE TO PROVIDE SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT, FIDUCIARY AND CHARITABLE GIVING SERVICES FOR TAX-EXEMPT QUAKER ORGANIZATIONS. INVESTMENTS ARE MADE ACCORDING TO OUR SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE INVESTMENT CRITERIA, GUIDED BY FRIENDS TESTIMONIES AND VALUES. AS A NON-PROFIT, WE SEEK SIMPLY TO COVER OUR OWN COSTS AND WORK TO INCORPORATE ECONOMIES OF SCALE AND SOUND BUSINESS PRINCIPLES IN ALL OUR OPERATIONS.
IRS990.Desc: INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT SERVICES:FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION PROVIDES PRUDENT, COST EFFECTIVE, INSTITUTIONAL INVESTMENT SERVICES FOR OVER 450 FRIENDS MEETINGS, CHURCHES, SCHOOLS AND ORGANIZATIONS. OUR SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE INVESTMENT ACTIVITIES INCLUDE SCREENING COMPANIES, ACTIVE VOTING OF PROXIES AND SHAREHOLDER ADVOCACY, SUCH AS SHAREHOLDER RESOLUTIONS AND COMPANY DIALOGUES - ALL GUIDED BY OUR QUAKER TESTIMONIES, VALUES AND CONCERNS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: GRANTMAKING:FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION SERVES AS FIDUCIARY/TRUSTEE FOR OVER 100 FUNDS RECEIVED UNDER WILL OR GIFT AGREEMENT. THESE FUNDS MAY BENEFIT SPECIFIC FRIENDS ORGANIZATIONS OR BE AVAILABLE FOR GRANTS TO FURTHER THE WORK OF FRIENDS. IN 2021, OVER 150 GRANTS WERE MADE TO CHARITIES. THESE GRANTS SUPPORTED THE WORK OF FRIENDS SCHOOLS, RETIREMENT COMMUNITIES, MEETINGS, AND OTHER CHARITABLE PURPOSES OF CONCERN TO THE RELIGIOUS SOCIETY OF FRIENDS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: PLANNED GIVING:FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION PROVIDES PLANNED GIVING ADMINISTRATIVE SERVICES. FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION HAS ISSUED 500 CHARITABLE GIFT ANNUITIES TO BENEFIT THE WORK OF QUAKER ORGANIZATIONS WITHIN THE RELIGIOUS SOCIETY OF FRIENDS AND THE CONCERNS OF ITS MEMBERS. IN 2021, GIFTS TOTALING OVER $1.9 MILLION WERE RECEIVED TO ESTABLISH CHARITABLE GIFT ANNUITIES, WHICH WILL EVENTUALLY BENEFIT A VARIETY OF QUAKER ORGANIZATIONS WORKING IN AREAS OF EDUCATION, PEACE, SOCIAL JUSTICE AND OTHER CHARITABLE PURPOSES OF IMPORTANCE TO FRIENDS.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: DIRECTOR OF BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT & CONSTITUENT REL

SupportedOrganizationName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: ALTERNATIVES TO VIOLENCE PROJECT USA INC
BIBLE ASSOCIATION OF FRIENDS IN AME
COMMUNITY FINANCIAL & CREDIT COUNSELING SERVICES INC
FRIENDS ASSOCATION OF SERVICES FOR THE ELDERLY
FRIENDS CEMETERY ASSOCIATION OF THE TOWN OF LEDYARD NY
FRIENDS WORLD COMMITTEE FOR CONSULTATION WORLD OFFICE
INCORPORATED TRUSTEES OF ORLANDO RELIGIOUS SOCIETY OF FRIENDS
LINDA & GENE FARLEY CENTER FOR PEACE JUSTICE & SUSTAINABILITY
LIVE OAK FRIENDS MEETING OF HOUSTON INC
MONTHLY MTG OF FRIENDS OF PHILADELP
THE FIDUCIARY TRUSTEES OF OHIO YEAR

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS ARE DESIGNATED BY CLASS AND PURPOSE IN THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS. FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION IS ORGANIZED EXCLUSIVELY FOR RELIGIOUS AND CHARITABLE PURPOSES, "TO RECEIVE, HOLD, MANAGE AND INVEST PROPERTY, AS TRUSTEE, CUSTODIAN OR AGENT, ON BEHALF OF PHILADELPHIA YEARLY MEETING OF THE RELIGIOUS SOCIETY OF FRIENDS, ANY OF ITS CONSTITUENT MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, HALF-YEARLY OR PREPARATIVE MEETINGS, INCLUDING COMMITTEES THEREOF AND "ON BEHALF OF OTHER ORGANIZATIONS WHICH QUALIFY AS ORGANIZATIONS OF THE TYPE DESCRIBED IN SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE OF 1954, OR IN THE CORRESPONDING PROVISION OF ANY SUBSEQUENT" EXCEPT THAT ALL SUCH ORGANIZATIONS ARE QUAKER OR QUAKER AFFILIATED CHARITABLE ORGANIZATIONS.

ALL MEMBERS OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS ARE MEMBERS OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION'S SUPPORTED CONSTITUENT ORGANIZATIONS OR OFFICERS, DIRECTORS OR TRUSTEES OF ONE OR MORE OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION'S SUPPORTED CONSTITUENT ORGANIZATIONS. THE INVESTMENT POLICY OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION IS APPROVED BY THE FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THE ANNUAL BUDGET WHICH REFLECTS THE PRIORITIES OF THE USE OF THE INCOME AND ASSETS OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION IS APPROVED BY FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION, THE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION, PROVIDES SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE INVESTMENT SERVICES, WHICH SPECIFICALLY INCORPORATE QUAKER RELIGIOUS VALUES IN THE INVESTMENT PROCESS, TO THE SUPPORTED QUAKER ORGANIZATIONS. THESE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED AT COST TO THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS. EACH SUPPORTED ORGANIZATION BENEFITS FROM THE ECONOMIES OF SCALE, THE PROFESSIONAL OPERATIONS AND OVERSIGHT AND THE FIDELITY OF IMPLEMENTATION OF QUAKER VALUES IN THE INVESTMENT PROCESS BY THE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION. MANY OF THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS WOULD BE UNABLE TO ATTAIN THESE BENEFITS WITHOUT THE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION. SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS ARE IDENTIFIED ON SCHEDULE A, PART I, LINE G.

THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS HAVE FINANCIAL ASSETS THAT NEED TO BE INVESTED. IF THE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION WERE NOT COMPLETING THIS FUNCTION THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS WOULD BE REQUIRED TO DO SO ON THEIR OWN AND WITHOUT THE BENEFITS FROM THE ECONOMIES OF SCALE, THE PROFESSIONAL OPERATIONS AND OVERSIGHT, AND THE EXPERT IMPLEMENTATION OF QUAKER VALUES IN THE INVESTMENT PROCESS BY THE SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION. THE PLANNED GIVING SERVICES ARE OFFERED TO THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS THAT DO NOT HAVE THE STAFF AND RESOURCES TO MANAGE AND ADMINISTER A PLANNED GIVING EFFORT TO SUPPORT THEIR ORGANIZATIONS. THE REMAINDER OF GIFTS MANAGED BY FFC DIRECTLY SUPPORT THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS.

FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION SERVES AS TRUSTEE FOR A NUMBER OF TRUSTS IN ITS ROLE AS SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION FOR PHILADELPHIA YEARLY MEETING (PYM). DISTRIBUTION COMMITTEES FOR THESE TRUSTS, WHICH ARE SELECTED BY THE SUPPORTED ORGANIZATION (PYM), RECOMMEND GRANTS, SUBJECT TO APPROVAL BY FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION, TO ORGANIZATIONS, SOME OF WHICH ARE NOT SUPPORTED ORGANIZATIONS. IN ADDITION FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION IS A SPONSORING ORGANIZATION FOR DONOR ADVISED FUNDS. DONOR ADVISORS MAY RECOMMEND GRANTS, SUBJECT TO APPROVAL BY FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION, TO A VARIETY OF ORGANIZATIONS.

OtherLiabilitiesOrgGrp.Desc: ASSETS HELD FOR BENEFIT OF OTHERS

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: INTENDED USE OF ENDOWMENT FUND THE ENDOWMENTS PROVIDE FINANCIAL SUPPORT FOR EDUCATION; HEATING AND CLOTHING; AND OTHER PURPOSES.

UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS UNDER ASC 740 GAAP REQUIRES ENTITIES TO EVALUATE, MEASURE, RECOGNIZE AND DISCLOSE ANY UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS. GAAP PRESCRIBES A MINIMUM THRESHOLD THAT A TAX POSITION IS REQUIRED TO MEET IN ORDER TO BE RECOGNIZED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. THE ORGANIZATION BELIEVES THAT IT HAD NO UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS AS DEFINED IN GAAP.
CHANGE IN BENEFICIAL INTEREST IN PERPETUAL TRUST 9,423. CHANGE IN MARKET VALUE OF GIFT ANNUITY AGREEMENTS 83,256.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: CHARLES A MELTON ARTS & EDUCATION CENTER

SCHOLARSHIPS FOR FINANCIAL AID FOR ACADEMICALLY TALENTED CHILDREN

USAddress.AddressLine1Txt: DEPT W NATL PROCESSING CTR PO BOX

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: SOLAR INSTALLATION FOR HOMELESS RESOURCE CENTER
PROVIDE SUPPORT FOR FRIENDS SOCIAL SERVICE ORGANIZATIONS

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: THE COVE CENTER FOR GRIEVING CHILDREN

RecipientTable.PurposeOfGrantTxt: CLASS OF '72 50TH REUNION FOR KAESEMEYER FULL-ACCESS FUND

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FFC FUNDS THAT MAKE GRANTS TO ORGANIZATIONS FALL UNDER TWO CATEGORIES. THE FIRST GROUP OF FUNDS MAKE GRANTS TO DESIGNATED ORGANIZATION(S) ON AN ONGOING BASIS. THE DESIGNATED RECIPIENTS OF THOSE GRANTS ARE ASKED TO REPORT ON A PERIODIC BASIS DIRECTLY TO THE CHARITABLE SERVICES COMMITTEE, A STANDING COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. OTHER FUNDS USE DISTRIBUTION COMMITTEES TO REVIEW GRANT APPLICATIONS 1-3 TIMES PER YEAR. THESE DISTRIBUTION COMMITTEES REPORT PERIODICALLY TO THE CHARITABLE SERVICES COMMITTEE AND, IN TURN, THE DISTRIBUTION COMMITTEES ASK THAT GRANTEES PROVIDE THE DISTRIBUTION COMMITTEE WITH A REPORT ON THE GRANTEE'S USE OF THE GRANT WITHIN A SPECIFIED TIME PERIOD, NORMALLY A YEAR AFTER THE GRANT IS RECEIVED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: BUSINESS RELATIONSHIPS AMONG BOARD MEMBERS CHRISTIE DUNCAN-TESSMER IS GENERAL SECRETARY FOR PHILADELPHIA YEARLY MEETING ("PYM"). PYM IS THE NAMED SUPPORTED ORGANIZATION OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GOVERNING BODY REVIEW OF FORM 990 THE FORM 990 IS REVIEWED ANNUALLY BY THE AUDIT COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD. IN ADDITION, A COPY OF THE FORM 990 IS SENT ELECTRONICALLY TO ALL MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR THEIR REVIEW BEFORE IT IS FILED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: MONITORING AND ENFORCEMENT OF CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY BOARD MEMBERS AND OFFICERS ARE REQUIRED TO COMPLETE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST QUESTIONNAIRE EACH YEAR IN WHICH THEY MUST DISCLOSE SUCH INTERESTS THAT MIGHT GIVE RISE TO POTENTIAL CONFLICTS. THE COMPLETED QUESTIONNAIRES ARE REVIEWED BY THE GOVERNANCE COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD. BOARD MEMBERS AND/OR STAFF MAY IDENTIFY POTENTIAL CONFLICT SITUATIONS. WHEN A CONFLICT IS IDENTIFIED, THE BOARD OR STAFF MEMBER RECUSES THEMSELVES FROM THE DECISION MAKING PROCESS ON THE MATTER THAT COULD POTENTIALLY REPRESENT A CONFLICT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PROCESS FOR DETERMINING COMPENSATION THE HUMAN RESOURCES COMMITTEE AND THE BOARD CHAIR ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE EVALUATION OF THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR. THIS INCLUDES AN ANNUAL FORMAL PERFORMANCE APPRAISAL AND THE DETERMINATION OF APPROPRIATE COMPENSATION. BOTH THE CALCULATION AND ANY CHANGE IN COMPENSATION IS APPROVED BY THE BOARD. INFORMATION IS OBTAINED FROM SURVEYS OF COMPENSATION AT OTHER COMPARABLE NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS. THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR EVALUATES ALL OTHER STAFF; THE EVALUATIONS AND ANY CHANGES IN COMPENSATION ARE REVIEWED BY THE HUMAN RESOURCES COMMITTEE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PUBLIC AVAILABILITY OF OTHER DOCUMENTS THE ANNUAL AUDIT OF FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION'S QUAKER GROWTH AND INCOME FUND AND QUAKER INDEX FUND IS DISTRIBUTED TO CONSTITUENT ORGANIZATIONS INVESTING IN THE FUND AND TO PROSPECTIVE INVESTOR ORGANIZATIONS UPON REQUEST. THE CORPORATION'S AUDIT IS PROVIDED TO ALL STATES WHERE REQUIRED AND IS ALSO AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST. FRIENDS FIDUCIARY CORPORATION'S GOVERNANCE STANDARDS AND INVESTMENT PHILOSOPHY AND GUIDANCE POLICIES ARE POSTED ON ITS WEBSITE AND ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.
CHANGE IN BENEFICIAL INTEREST IN PERPETUAL TRUST 9,423. CHANGE IN MARKET VALUE OF GIFT ANNUITY AGREEMENTS 83,256.
